name: Zappa (Serverless Python on AWS Lambda)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Zappa contributors / Rich Jones

auth.notes: Uses AWS credentials (env or ~/.aws/credentials) for deployment

agent_readiness.notes: Deploy WSGI Python apps (Django, Flask, Falcon) to AWS Lambda + API Gateway. zappa init → generates zappa_settings.json. zappa deploy production. zappa update production. zappa_settings.json: {'production': {'app_function': 'myapp.app', 's3_bucket': 'my-bucket', 'aws_region': 'us-east-1'}}. zappa tail production (CloudWatch logs). zappa schedule for cron tasks. zappa undeploy. Handles packaging, S3 upload, Lambda function creation, API Gateway setup automatically. Django: app_function = 'wsgi.application'. VPC support. Custom domain: 'domain': 'api.example.com'. async_response for long tasks via SQS. Maintenance mode — development very slow.

gotchas: Effectively in maintenance mode — PRs slow to merge
Django/Flask only — not async frameworks (FastAPI needs workarounds)
Package size limits on Lambda (250MB unzipped) can be hit
Cold start times significant for API use cases
Complex VPC and IAM configuration often needed
Mangum is the modern alternative for async Python on Lambda
best_when: Existing Flask/Django apps needing quick Lambda deployment
When AWS Chalice is too opinionated
avoid_when: New serverless Python projects — use AWS SAM, Mangum, or Chalice
FastAPI or async frameworks (use Mangum adapter instead)
When container-based Lambda deployment is available
summary: Zappa deploys WSGI Python (Flask, Django) to AWS Lambda + API Gateway. zappa init; zappa deploy production; zappa update. Handles packaging, S3 upload, Lambda + API Gateway config. MIT. In maintenance mode. Best for existing Flask/Django serverless deployment. Not for async frameworks — use Mangum. Cold starts significant. AWS SAM or Chalice for new projects.
